title: Palicourea oblita
intro: Palicourea oblita is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical regions of South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to its ability to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Palicourea oblita include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which have been identified as the primary sources of its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Palicourea oblita is often prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in the form of a tincture or extract, making it a valuable resource for natural remedy seekers.
taxonomy: Palicourea oblita is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This genus is part of the Rubiales order within the asterid clade of flowering plants. The family Rubiaceae is a large and diverse group containing over 13,000 species. The Rubiales order is a clade of flowering plants that includes the families Rubiaceae, Theaceae, and Styracaceae. The plant kingdom classification for Palicourea oblita would be: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Order: Rubiales, Family: Rubiaceae, Genus: Palicourea, Species: P. oblita.

side_effects: Palicourea oblita is a plant species that has been reported to have potential side effects due to its toxic compounds.
